description: Promotional content offering complimentary items or discounts with food purchase, leveraging value-stacking to drive orders and repeat visits.
structural_recipe: Lead with attention-grabbing emoji and casual tone (ثلوثيتنا غير). State the main offer clearly (main dish purchase). Highlight the free add-on or discount (complimentary soup/side). Use call-to-action imperative (اطلب). Close with relevant branded hashtags referencing restaurant name or category. Keep caption under 100 characters for mobile scannability.
why_it_works: Saudi consumers respond strongly to perceived value and bundles; free add-ons feel like personal wins rather than discounts, triggering impulse orders. Casual Arabic dialect (Hijazi colloquialism) builds familiarity and trust. Emoji usage signals modern, approachable brand voice.
transplantation_caveats: Ensure compliance with SFDA food promotion regulations regarding claims of 'free' items
Value perception varies by price point—bundle must feel genuinely valuable not desperate
Casual dialect may alienate formal-leaning demographics; test audience first
